---
title: "Machine Learning Summer School 2026"
format:
  html:
    title-block: false
    toc: false
---

::: {.hub-hero}
# Welcome

::: {.hub-tagline}
Slides, **modules**, notebooks, and a short map of the week — all in one place.
:::

::: {.hub-lead}
Use this site **during and after class**: open today’s deck, then try the matching notebook or **[lab exercise](exercises/index.qmd)** when you feel like coding along. If something is unclear, that’s normal — bring it to the room or office hours.
:::
:::

::: {.callout-tip .hub-start appearance="simple"}
## Start here (2 minutes)

1. **Open today’s slides** — we follow **Day 1 (Monday) → Day 2 (Tuesday) → Day 4 (Thursday)** in class.
2. **When you’re ready**, open a **module** for a short guided page, or a **notebook** for a long IDE lab — same R you already use (RStudio or Positron is fine).
3. **Skim “Your week”** below for the story of the three days — no jargon quiz attached.
:::

::: {.hub-grid}
::: {.hub-card}
::: {.hub-pill}
Palmer Penguins
:::
### Our real-data running example

- **[About the dataset](dataset-penguins.qmd)** — variables, biology, caveats, and how we reuse penguins **Monday → Thursday**  
- Links to **penguin-only** notebooks from that page (mass, binary species, sex, three-class species); **`tidymodels`** walk-throughs live as **Module 04** (+ **07–08** follow-ups; see [module library](modules/index.qmd)).

Synthetic **gene** notebooks are there so we **know the true story behind the data** (which “genes” really matter) while we teach LASSO, ridge, and AIC.
:::

::: {.hub-card}
::: {.hub-pill}
Class slides
:::
### Follow along day by day

- **[Day 1 (Monday)](slides/day-01-monday.qmd)** — from questions to simple models: when a straight line (or curve) helps, and when a model is “trying too hard.”  
- **[Day 2 (Tuesday)](slides/day-02-tuesday.qmd)** — branching decisions (trees), and how to keep your code organized so Day 4 still makes sense.  
- **[Day 4 (Thursday)](slides/day-04-thursday.qmd)** — forests, boosting, neural nets, PCA/imputation in recipes, variable importance, and evaluation.  

Each deck links back here from the **footer** if you get lost.
:::

::: {.hub-card}
::: {.hub-pill}
Hands-on notebooks
:::
### Try the code yourself

- **[Notebook home](notebooks/index.qmd)** — long `.Rmd` labs (**classical** `lm` / `glm` / `rpart` / `glmnet` on genes + penguins)  
- **`tidymodels` on penguins (site modules):** [Module 04](modules/module-04-pipeline.qmd#train-test-last-fit) (train/test, then [Day 2 pipeline](modules/module-04-pipeline.qmd#canonical-pipeline-tuesday)), [07](modules/module-07-penguins-choose-metrics.qmd) (metrics), [08](modules/module-08-penguins-compare-models.qmd) (compare RF / XGB / MLP)  
- **Genes (simulation where we know the answers):** [linear + LASSO](notebooks/linear-regression-lasso.Rmd), [logistic + tree](notebooks/logistic-regression-gene-disease.Rmd)  
- **Palmer Penguins (notebooks):** [body mass](notebooks/penguins-body-mass.Rmd), [Adelie vs Gentoo](notebooks/penguins-classification.Rmd), [sex](notebooks/penguins-sex-classification.Rmd), [three species](notebooks/penguins-species-multiclass.Rmd)  

Go as fast or slow as you like; notebooks and modules are for **you**, not for a grade on this site.
:::

::: {.hub-card}
::: {.hub-pill}
Lab exercises
:::
### Afternoon practicals (with solutions)

- **[Lab exercises home](exercises/index.qmd)** — task handbook plus links to every **solution** notebook (HTML + source)  
- **[Full handbook](exercises/mlss2026-lab-exercises.qmd)** — Day 1 genes, Day 2 & 4 microbiome, data/model card templates  
- No grade on this site: try the tasks yourself first; solutions are there when you need them.
:::

::: {.hub-card}
::: {.hub-pill}
Outline & modules
:::
### If you want more structure

- **[Curriculum outline](curriculum-outline.qmd)** — how the three days connect (also useful if you miss a session)  
- **[Module library](modules/index.qmd)** — eight topic pages: **01–06** concepts + **04** canonical `tidymodels` pipeline (train/test beat + same include as Day 2 slides); **07–08** short **`tidymodels`** labs (metrics while tuning; compare learners). See [modules vs notebooks](modules/index.qmd#modules-vs-notebooks) on that page for how we split formats.  
:::
:::

::: {.hub-week}
## Your week at a glance

::: {.hub-week-intro}
Think of it as one story in three chapters: **see the pattern**, **keep your work honest**, then **choose and explain** models without pretending they’re magic. Curiosity beats perfection.
:::

- **Day 1 (Monday)** — predictions, when a model is “too wiggly,” and gentle introductions to linear models, logistic regression, and a bit of regularization. You’ll see **simulated genes** (we scripted the biology so we know what’s true) and **Palmer Penguins** (real measurements) side by side.  
- **Day 2 (Tuesday)** — decision trees (“if bill length is above this line, go left…”) and how to **fit and check models in a tidy, repeatable way** in R so Day 4 still makes sense.  
- **Day 3 (Wednesday)** — mini symposium and social event (no lecture deck on this site).  
- **Day 4 (Thursday)** — stronger models, what to watch when outcomes aren’t balanced, and how to discuss **why** a model leaned one way — without claiming it “knows” biology or medicine by itself.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" appearance="simple"}
## For instructors & TAs

- **Local preview:** from this project folder run `quarto preview` so links resolve; after `quarto render`, you can serve `_site/` with any static file server.  

- **Course design detail** — the [curriculum outline](curriculum-outline.qmd) describes the two-track story (genes + Palmer Penguins), calibration, causal guardrails around explainability, data/model cards, reproducibility, and assessment prompts.  
- **Site output** lives in `_site/`; GitHub Actions builds the same way for Pages.  
- **Palmer Penguins** — [About the dataset](dataset-penguins.qmd); Day 2 (Tuesday) **modeling pipeline** examples use the same **Adelie vs Gentoo** setup as the penguin classification notebook (implemented with the `tidymodels` R packages).  

- **Single source for the canonical penguin pipeline** — [`_includes/day02-tidymodels-walkthrough.qmd`](_includes/day02-tidymodels-walkthrough.qmd) (Day 2 slides + Module 04 via `tidymodels-pipeline.qmd` wrapper).
- **Slide reveal mode** — edit `incremental` in [`slides/_reveal-config.yml`](slides/_reveal-config.yml) (`false` = full slide at once; `true` = step-by-step bullets), then re-render the day deck.
- **Module reading (L1/L2, lasso, elastic net)** — [Module 02](modules/module-02-regularization.qmd) with [slide deep-links](modules/index.qmd#module-02-regularization).
:::
